Package component.modifier.combinator

Class Absorber

java.lang.Object
component.modifier.Modifier
component.modifier.combinator.Combinator
component.modifier.combinator.Absorber
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
public class Absorber extends Combinator
The Absorber is a stateful Combinator that transfers the identity of one card to another.

The process occurs in two phases:

  1. Phase 1: The first card to enter is "absorbed" (removed from the board) and stored internally.
  2. Phase 2: The next card to enter inherits the numerical value of the absorbed card.

  • Constructor Details

    • Absorber

      public Absorber()

  • Method Details

    • modify

      public void modify(Card toModify)
      Handles the two-step modification logic.

      This method respects the standard immunities:

      • Modifier.checkSetDisable(component.card.Card): Checks for Corrupted material.
      • Modifier.checkDestroyGlass(component.card.Card): Checks if a Glass card should shatter.
      • Combinator.checkStone(component.card.Card): Checks if a Stone card is immune to combinators.
      Specified by:
      modify in class Modifier
      Parameters:
      toModify - The card currently entering the Absorber's tile.

    • isBlocking

      public boolean isBlocking()
      Returns:
      false, allowing cards to move into this tile freely.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets the modifier, re-enabling it and clearing any stored card buffer.
      Overrides:
      reset in class Combinator

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Generates a dynamic tooltip.

      If a card is currently absorbed, the tooltip will explicitly state which card is being "held" in the buffer using Tooltip.ref(Object).

      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Overrides:
      getTooltip in class Combinator
      Returns:
      A Tooltip detailing the absorber's state and function.
